response:
housekeeping manager oversees 100 rooms in a hotel and collaborates with the concierge to address guest concerns. Each day a certain percentage of rooms require attention due to various issues such as cleaning, maintenance, or guest requests. On average 20% of rooms need attention. The concierge assists in addressing 40% of these concerns by coordinating with other departments.  

text  
piece  
piece  
highlighted section  
the number of rooms requiring attention on a given day follows a Poisson distribution with a mean of 20 the probability that exactly 15 rooms need attention on that day is calculated as e^(-20) * 20^15 / 15!  
highlighted section  
the effectiveness of the concierge's assistance can be modeled by a success rate of solving 90% of the concerns they address if the concierge assists with 8 concerns on a particular day the probability that at least 7 of these concerns are resolved successfully is calculated as 1 - (1 - 0.9)^8
